steps.reveal: Audentes = the bold/daring (acc. pl., object of 'iuvat')

hints.nominative: While 'audentes' could be nominative, here it is accusative — fortune helps 'the bold'
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'audentibus' — 'audentes' is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'audentium' — 'audentes' is accusative, object of 'iuvat'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'audentis' ends in -is (3rd declension participle)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'audentis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'audentis' ends in -is

hints.ablative: While 'fortuna' could be ablative, here it is the nominative subject of 'iuvat'
hints.vocative: Vocative looks the same in 1st decl., but 'fortuna' is the subject here, not addressed

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'fortunae' ends in -ae (1st declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'fortunae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'fortunae' ends in -ae

hints.to judge: That would be 'iudicare' — 'iuvat' comes from 'iuvare' (to help)
hints.to swear: That would be 'iurare' — 'iuvat' comes from 'iuvare' (to help)
hints.to order: That would be 'iubere' — 'iuvat' comes from 'iuvare' (to help)

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'iuvit' — 'iuvat' with -a- is present indicative (1st conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'iuvet' — 'iuvat' with -a- is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'iuvabat' — 'iuvat' is present indicative

hints.iubeo, iubere, iussi, iussum: These are the parts of 'iubere' (to order)
hints.iuro, iurare, iuravi, iuratum: These are the parts of 'iurare' (to swear)
hints.iudico, iudicare, iudicavi, iudicatum: These are the parts of 'iudicare' (to judge)
